name: 💥AI Social Video Generator with GPT-4, Kling & Blotato —Auto-Post to Instagram, Facebook,, TikTok...
description: Automated workflow: 💥AI Social Video Generator with GPT-4, Kling & Blotato —Auto-Post to Instagram, Facebook,, TikTok, Twitter & Pinterest - vide. This workflow integrates 12 different services: telegramTrigger, stickyNote, httpRequest, wait, telegram. It contains 81 nodes and follows best practices for error handling and security.
shortDescription: 💥AI Social Video Generator with GPT-4, Kling & Blotato —Auto-Post to Instagram, Facebook,, TikTok... workflow containing 38 nodes.

originalWorkflowJson.name: 💥AI Social Video Generator with GPT-4, Kling & Blotato —Auto-Post to Instagram, Facebook,, TikTok...

nodes.name: Sticky Note

parameters.content:
# 🟫 STEP 1 — Create Video Using AI
## This step handles the full video creation pipeline using AI.
### It starts from a Telegram message containing a prompt, 
### refines the prompt with GPT-4 to suit Kling’s video engine, 
### and generates a cinematic video based entirely on text input.

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note1

parameters.content:
# 🟫 STEP 2 — Add Voice-Over to Video
## Here, a short-form voice-over script is generated using GPT-4 based on the topic.
### The script is converted to speech, uploaded, and merged with the AI-generated video — resulting in a fully narrated visual asset.

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note3

parameters.content:
# 🟥 STEP 5 — Auto-Publish to 9 Social Platforms
## The final step automates distribution using Blotato’s API.
## The video is auto-published to Instagram, YouTube, TikTok, Facebook, 
## LinkedIn, Threads, Twitter (X), Bluesky, and Pinterest 
## — all in one go, with no manual work required.

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note4

parameters.content:
# 🟫 STEP 4 — Save Video & Notify via Telegram
## This step generates a title and caption for the video, 
## saves the content metadata to a Google Sheet for future tracking, 
### And sends both the final video and its description to a Telegram chat for validation or reuse.
### The script is converted to speech, uploaded, and merged with the AI-generated video — resulting in a fully narrated visual asset.

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note2

parameters.content:
# 🟫 STEP 3 — Add Captions to Enhance Engagement
## To increase accessibility and boost social engagement, 
## this step overlays professional-looking subtitles on the video using a styling template.
### This results in a final video that includes visuals, voice-over, and captions.

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.notes: This code node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Transform Prompt for Kling (GPT-4)

options.systemMessage:
=⚙️ System Instructions – AI Agent:
You are a prompt optimization assistant for Kling AI, a cutting-edge text-to-video generation platform.

🎯 Mission:
Transform brief user inputs into cinematic, high-quality video prompts rich in sensory details, spatial context, and dynamic motion—perfectly tuned for Kling AI.

✍️ Instructions:
Expand short ideas into visually immersive scenes.

Use concrete visual elements: colors, textures, lighting, motion, atmosphere, camera angles.

Keep descriptions concise but vivid, like a scene from a movie script.

Output only the final prompt—no explanations, no formatting tips.

🌟 Examples:
User Input: “Rainy street at night”
Final Prompt:
Neon signs reflect in puddles on a dimly lit city street. Raindrops ripple across a parked motorcycle's chrome. A figure in a trench coat walks past flickering lights as steam rises from a nearby sewer grate. The camera tracks from ground level, capturing water splashing underfoot.

User Input: “Mountain hike”
Final Prompt:
A hiker climbs a rugged mountain trail at sunrise, golden light hitting snow-capped peaks in the distance. Wind rustles through pine trees as birds soar overhead. The camera pans slowly from behind, revealing a vast valley below bathed in morning mist.

User Input: “Cozy library”
Final Prompt:
A warm library room with wooden shelves lined with old books. Dust floats in the sunbeams streaming through tall windows. A cat naps on a leather armchair while a hand flips through a weathered novel. The camera gently rotates around the room, revealing small glowing lamps.

nodes.notes: This agent node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Generate Voice-Over Script

values.content:
=Task:
Craft a short-form voice-over script that perfectly fits a 7-second video duration. The script should align with the following topic:

{{ $('Extract Prompt & Caption').item.json.captionIdea }}

Requirements:

Match the average spoken word count for a 7-second voice-over (approximately 18–22 words, depending on pacing).

The script must be tight, impactful, and written in a natural spoken tone suitable for narration.

No intro text or labels — return only the plain voice-over script with no headers or commentary.

✅ Example (For Context Only):
Topic: "Why cold showers boost productivity"
Generated Output:
Cold showers shock your system awake, boost focus, and kickstart circulation—training your brain to embrace discomfort.

nodes.name: Generate Social Caption from Voiceover

values.content:
=📲 Prompt: Caption Generator for Voiceover-Based Social Posts
Your Role:
Create a concise and engaging social media caption that builds directly on the voiceover script below:
{{ $('Generate Voice-Over Script').item.json.message.content }}

🧠 Caption Guidelines:
Keep it short, compelling, and value-driven.

Avoid generic motivational fluff — focus on real, actionable insight or highlight a problem/solution pattern.


Structure:

One sentence per line.

Note: Do not use this character: " in the result.
Return a single short paragraph with no line breaks and no special characters.
Note: Do not use this character: " in the result.

nodes.notes: This openAi node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Generate YouTube-Style Title

values.content:
=Act as a YouTube Title Expert.
Based on the following video description:
{{ $('Generate Voice-Over Script').item.json.message.content }}
Generate a short, punchy, and curiosity-driven YouTube video title that makes people want to click.
Make it feel urgent, valuable, or surprising — and avoid generic or boring phrases.
Keep it under 70 characters. Return only the title, no explanations.
Note: The title must be free of special characters and the character ". Return only a plain text title.

parameters.content:
# 🟫 STEP 1 — Create Video Using AI
## This step handles the full video creation pipeline using AI.
### It starts from a Telegram message containing a prompt, 
### refines the prompt with GPT-4 to suit Kling’s video engine, 
### and generates a cinematic video based entirely on text input.

# 🟫 STEP 2 — Add Voice-Over to Video
## Here, a short-form voice-over script is generated using GPT-4 based on the topic.
### The script is converted to speech, uploaded, and merged with the AI-generated video — resulting in a fully narrated visual asset.

# 🟫 STEP 3 — Add Captions to Enhance Engagement
## To increase accessibility and boost social engagement, 
## this step overlays professional-looking subtitles on the video using a styling template.
### This results in a final video that includes visuals, voice-over, and captions.

# 🟥 STEP 5 — Auto-Publish to 9 Social Platforms
## The final step automates distribution using Blotato’s API.
## The video is auto-published to Instagram, YouTube, TikTok, Facebook, 
## LinkedIn, Threads, Twitter (X), Bluesky, and Pinterest 
## — all in one go, with no manual work required.

# 🟫 STEP 4 — Save Video & Notify via Telegram
## This step generates a title and caption for the video, 
## saves the content metadata to a Google Sheet for future tracking, 
### And sends both the final video and its description to a Telegram chat for validation or reuse.
### The script is converted to speech, uploaded, and merged with the AI-generated video — resulting in a fully narrated visual asset.

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: open Ai  A P I details
google Sheets  O Auth2  A P I details
configurationSteps: Verify the settings of the trigger node "Trigger: Telegram Prompt".
Open the "Sticky Note" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note1" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note3" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note4" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note2" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Transform Prompt for Kling (GPT-4)" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "OpenAI Model Bridge" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Generate Video via Kling API" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Wait for Video Generation" node (n8n-nodes-base.wait) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Get Generated Video URL" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Generate Voice-Over Script" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Convert Script to Audio (TTS)" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Upload Audio to Cloudinary" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Merge Audio + Video" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Wait for Audio/Video Fusion" node (n8n-nodes-base.wait) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Get Video URL with Audio" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Wait Before Captioning" node (n8n-nodes-base.wait) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Add Captions/Subtitles to Video" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Wait for Caption Render" node (n8n-nodes-base.wait) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Get Final Video URL (Audio + Captions)" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Generate Social Caption from Voiceover" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Generate YouTube-Style Title" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Save Video Metadata to Google Sheets" Google Sheets node.
Ensure you select the spreadsheet file and specific tab/sheet from the dropdown menus.
Map the input JSON keys to your target sheet columns.
Open the "Send Final Video to Telegram" node (n8n-nodes-base.telegram) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Send Caption Link via Telegram" node (n8n-nodes-base.telegram) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Upload Video to Blotato" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Post to Instagram" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Post to YouTube" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Post to TikTok" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Post to Facebook Page" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Post to Threads" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Post to Twitter (X)" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Post to LinkedIn" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Post to Bluesky" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Post to Pinterest" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Assign Social Media IDs".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
